title: 2022 Wakefield Trinity season
wikitext:
{{Short description|Wakefield Trinity season}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2022}} {{Infobox rugby league team season | year = 2022 | team_colour = #21145F; border: solid #B50000 2px | font_colour = white; | league = Super League | league_link = | league_rank = 10 | league_wins = 10 | league_draws = 0 | league_losses = 17 | points_for = 519 | points_against = 696 | playoff_result = | cup = Challenge Cup | cup_result = Quarter-finals | cup_wins = 1 | cup_losses = 1 | CEO_title = Chairman | CEO = Michael Carter | coach_title = Head Coach | coach = [[Willie Poching]] | captain = [[Jacob Miller (rugby league)|Jacob Miller]] | stadium = [[Belle Vue (Wakefield)|Belle Vue]]
[[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire]] | avg_attendance = 4,552 | agg_attendance = 59,991 | high_attendance = 7,046 | low_attendance = 3,221 | top_tries = [[Lewis Murphy (rugby league)|Lewis Murphy]] (19) | top_goals = [[Mason Lino]] (47) | top_points = Mason Lino (102) | prev_season = [[2021 Wakefield Trinity season|2021]] | next_season = [[2023 Wakefield Trinity season|2023]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Wakefield Trinity seasons }} The '''2022 Wakefield Trinity season''' is the 24th consecutive season in the [[Super League]] and their 127th season at the [[Belle Vue (Wakefield)|Belle Vue]]. Wakefield were coached by [[Willie Poching]] and they competed in both [[Super League XXVII]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. ==Season review== Wakefield's season got off to a poor start when they lost their first pre-season friendly 34–6 to [[Leeds Rhinos]] and were subsequently fined for a breach of operational rules regarding the RFL Covid-19 policy.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36659/breach-of-rfl-operational-rules-wakefield-trinity |title=Breach of RFL Operational Rules: Wakefield Trinity |publisher=RFL |date=22 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} On 13 February, Wakefield began their Super League campaign with a 16–12 loss to [[Hull F.C.]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} which was followed by a 24–22 loss at [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} Wakefield suffered two more losses before gaining their first win of the season, an 18–12 victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36784/betfred-super-league-round-five-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round Five Round-Up|publisher=RFL |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} Then came two successful visits to [[Warrington Wolves]] for wins in the league and [[Challenge Cup]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36891/betfred-challenge-cup-round-6-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round 6 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=27 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and a victory over [[Salford Red Devils]] that moved Wakefield up to seventh in the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36949/betfred-super-league-round-7-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 7 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=3 April 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} Their winning run ended with a loss to [[Wigan Warriors]] in the quarter-finals of the Challenge Cup{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37006/betfred-challenge-cup-quarter-final-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup quarter-final round-up |publisher=RFL |date=10 April 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} with their next win not coming until early June against Hull F.C. by which time Wakefield had slipped to 11th in the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60197/betfred-super-league-round-14-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 14 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=5 June 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} At the [[Magic Weekend]], a 38–26 loss to Toulouse left Wakefield only two points from the bottom of the table,{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60469/betfred-super-league-round-18-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 18 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=11 July 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and a 15–10 loss to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the next round saw Wakefield drop below Toulouse on points difference.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60519/betfred-super-league-round-19-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 19 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=17 July 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} However, by the end of the month they had moved back out of the relegation spot with a 32–6 victory over [[Castleford Tigers]],{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60644/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-21|title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 21 |publisher=RFL |date=1 August 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and began moving clear of danger from mid-August with a 30–12 win over Wigan.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60738/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-23 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 23 |publisher=RFL |date=15 August 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} Wakefield came close to finishing off the season with a fifth consecutive win, but lost 14–16 to [[Huddersfield Giants]] in extra time.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} ==Results== {{clear}} ===Pre-season friendlies=== {{legend2|#CFC|Win|border=1px solid #000000|text=W}}
